pr.title: ftGameWatch - all files except SpecialN.c complete and matching
pr.author: VetriTheRetri

summary: Large Mr. Game & Watch fighter decompilation PR that moved most of ftgamewatch.s into matching C files, leaving SpecialN.c essentially undecompiled per the title. It added dedicated C translation units for Attack100, Attack11, AttackAir, AttackLw3, AttackS4, SpecialHi, SpecialLw, and SpecialS, wired them into obj_files.mk, expanded ftgamewatch.h with action-state IDs, special attributes, item callback prototypes, and named helper functions, and updated fighter/item/common headers needed by the new code. The PR also converted ftdata action callback tables to point at the new ftGameWatch_* C symbols and renamed several Ness action flag macros for consistency.
what_changed: Reduced asm/melee/ft/chara/ftgamewatch.s from a large 5550-line assembly body to a much smaller remainder, while adding thousands of lines of new C across ftGameWatch action files.
Added Game & Watch action C files for jab/rapid jab, aerials and landings, down tilt, forward smash, Judgement, Oil Panic, and Fire Rescue.
Added the new ftGameWatch C object files to obj_files.mk so the matching C replaces removed assembly in the build.

Expanded ftgamewatch.h from a minimal attribute declaration into the central Game & Watch header with include guards, action-state enum values starting at AS_FTCOMMON = 340, Oil Panic charge macros, action-state flag macros, special attribute layout, and many prototypes.

Added ftGameWatch item lifecycle names for move-spawned articles such as Torch, Manhole, Parachute, Turtle, Sparky, Greenhouse, Judgement, Panic, and Rescue, including setup, remove, damage, hitlag, and check callbacks.
Updated ftdata.s action tables to reference named ftGameWatch callback symbols such as ftGameWatch_Attack11_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll, ftGameWatch_AttackAirN_* and SpecialS/SpecialAirS callbacks.
Renamed struct SpecialAttrs_Gaw to struct SpecialAttrs_GameWatch and gave several Game & Watch special-attribute fields semantic names, including panic charge/damage and article GObj pointers.
Introduced ftGameWatchVars for action-local state at 0x2340/0x2344, including Attack11 and SpecialLw state.
Changed attr.x18C_Jab_2InputWindow from s32 to f32, which was likely required by matching code using that fighter attribute as a float.
Changed ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining return type from s32 to BOOL.
Renamed common controller macros from A_BUTTON_HELD/B_BUTTON_HELD to HSD_BUTTON_A/HSD_BUTTON_B.
Renamed It_Kind_GameWatch_Pesticide to It_Kind_GameWatch_Greenhouse with a more accurate comment for Game & Watch's insecticide spray.
Added ItemModStruct in item.h and HALF_PI64 in lbvector.h to support the new C.
Renamed Ness action flag macros from NESS_* to FTNESS_* and updated Ness source uses, apparently for fighter-prefix consistency.
smart_moves: Split the fighter work by move family rather than keeping all code in ftgamewatch.c, making a very large character decomp easier to review and search.
Kept SpecialN.c separate and effectively left it out of the claimed completion scope instead of overstating full fighter completion.
Promoted repeated raw Game & Watch action-state IDs into an ftGameWatchAction enum, making ftdata callback tables and action changes easier to correlate.
Named article ownership fields in SpecialAttrs_GameWatch as HSD_GObj* pointers, which makes item cleanup, hitlag forwarding, and damage callbacks more maintainable than raw u32 slots.
Codified action-state flag bundles such as FTGAMEWATCH_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG and FTGAMEWATCH_SPECIALLW_COLL_FLAG in the header, reducing repeated opaque bitmask expressions.
Used consistent suffixes for action callbacks: _Action, _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, _Coll, plus GroundToAir and AirToGround transition helpers.
Used item callback naming patterns that expose lifecycle intent: Setup, SetFlag, Remove, OnDamage, EnterHitlag, ExitHitlag, and ItemCheck*Remove.
Updated neighboring Ness macro names to FTNESS_* while adding FTGAMEWATCH_* names, avoiding character-specific prefix inconsistency in shared fighter code.
decomp_lessons: For item-heavy fighters, decompiling the spawned-article lifecycle alongside action states is important; Game & Watch moves manage many per-move article GObj pointers in fighter special attributes.
Large fighter assembly removals should be paired with ftdata callback rewrites so action-state tables name the new C callback symbols directly.
When a character has many custom action states, define a local action enum in the character header early; it makes Fighter_ActionStateChange calls and table entries much less error-prone.
Semantic field names in the Fighter special-attrs union can unlock broad matching work because subsequent C can use pointer and counter fields naturally instead of casts from raw offsets.
Header cleanup outside the target fighter may be necessary for matching, such as correcting BOOL return types, float-vs-int attribute fields, item enum names, or shared button macro names.
The title and diff indicate completion status should be scoped carefully: this PR completed all listed Game & Watch files except SpecialN.c, not the entire character including neutral special.
naming_conventions: Character action callback pattern: ftGameWatch_<Move>_Anim, ftGameWatch_<Move>_IASA, ftGameWatch_<Move>_Phys, ftGameWatch_<Move>_Coll.

Start action pattern for specials: ftGameWatch_SpecialS_StartAction, ftGameWatch_SpecialSAir_StartAction, ftGameWatch_SpecialHi_StartAction, ftGameWatch_SpecialHiAir_StartAction, ftGameWatch_SpecialLw_StartAction, ftGameWatch_SpecialLwAir_StartAction.
Ground/air transition pattern: ftGameWatch_<Move>_GroundToAir and ftGameWatch_<AirMove>_AirToGround.

Article lifecycle pattern: ftGameWatch_Item<Name>Setup, ftGameWatch_Item<Name>SetFlag, ftGameWatch_Item<Name>Remove, ftGameWatch_Item<Name>OnDamage, ftGameWatch_Item<Name>EnterHitlag, ftGameWatch_Item<Name>ExitHitlag, ftGameWatch_ItemCheck<Name>Remove.
Action-state constants use AS_GAMEWATCH_* with ground and air variants such as AS_GAMEWATCH_SPECIALS1 through AS_GAMEWATCH_SPECIALS9 and AS_GAMEWATCH_SPECIALAIRS1 through AS_GAMEWATCH_SPECIALAIRS9.
Character macro prefixes use FTGAMEWATCH_* and FTNESS_* rather than shorter or ambiguous NESS_* names.
Game & Watch special attributes use descriptive offset-preserving names such as x2238_panicCharge, x223C_panicDamage, x2248_manholeGObj, x2254_fireGObj, x2264_judgementGObj, and x226C_rescueGObj.
Item kind names should reflect move/article terminology; this PR changes GameWatch_Pesticide to GameWatch_Greenhouse.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly functions with C while leaving unresolved or excluded SpecialN assembly in place, reducing risk by limiting the claimed matching scope.
Used decomp.me scratch links in new C comments for individual functions, giving traceability for matching attempts.
Preserved offset-style struct field names while adding semantic suffixes, a common tactic to document layout without destabilizing known offsets.
Used inline helper functions in fighter.h, including getFighter and stickGetDir, to produce C forms usable across many new action functions.
Corrected types that affect codegen, including BOOL for ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining and f32 for x18C_Jab_2InputWindow.
Added HALF_PI64 as a double-precision constant in lbvector.h, likely to match floating-point constant generation in vector/angle-related code.
Introduced action-state flag macros to keep large Fighter_ActionStateChange flag bitmasks identical while making call sites more readable.
Updated ftdata.s callback pointers after C symbol creation, allowing data-table references to resolve to C functions instead of anonymous assembly labels.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that completes ftGameWatch_SpecialN.c and compare its naming and struct assumptions against PR 407.
Search for later changes to struct SpecialAttrs_GameWatch field names to see which x2240/x2244 fields were eventually identified.
Search later PRs for cleanup of FTGAMEWATCH action flag macros or replacement of raw FIGHTER_UNK flags with named meanings.
Check whether ItemModStruct was expanded after this PR and whether its initial one-field layout was sufficient.
Look for follow-up PRs involving Game & Watch article item files, especially Greenhouse, Manhole, Fire, Parachute, Turtle, Sparky, Judgement, Panic, and Rescue.
